key_metrics: Diluted EPS of $4.58, down slightly from $4.60 YoY
Gross profit of $15.13 billion, up 4.9% YoY
Operating expenses increased 8.7% YoY to $8.57 billion
qoq_changes: Cash and cash equivalents increased to $2.80 billion from $1.66 billion at the end of fiscal 2024
Merchandise inventories increased to $24.84 billion from $23.45 billion
Short-term debt decreased to zero from $316 million
new_risks: Integration and execution risks related to the pending GMS acquisition and recently acquired SRS Distribution
Macroeconomic pressures affecting housing, credit markets, and consumer demand
Potential impacts from tariffs, trade policy changes, or international trade disputes
management_tone: Neutral; while top-line sales demonstrated resilient growth of nearly 5%, rising SG&A and interest expenses compressed margins, leading to flat operating income and a slight dip in net earnings.
analyst_note: Home Depot delivered solid top-line growth in Q2 2025, but higher operating and interest expenses pressured the bottom line, resulting in a slight YoY decline in EPS. Focus remains on the successful integration of SRS and the pending GMS acquisition to drive future value.
